anomalyRuns.note: Radius and period tails are visible but bounded by archive selection.
Mass tail is sensitive to missingness and target selection.
Record metadata payloads vary enough that byte-size is not a clean scientific signal.
CMS metadata supports artifact discovery but not physics-level inference.
Dataset schema was usable; descriptor signal remains preliminary.
Small-domain target acted as a scale contrast rather than a robust anomaly.
Resolution fields are useful but missingness changes claim strength.
Entry-level metadata is replayable but not enough for broad structural claims.
Temporal residuals are visible but not a causal climate explanation.
Hemisphere contrast is a useful holdout target.
Prime gaps produced a non-trivial but known-style residual target.
Growth regularity is strong but expected; useful as a control.
